At present, BRAC University has 10 schools and 8 departments. Know More
Institutes
BRAC University hosts 3 institutes. Know More
Centres
There are 4 centres at BRAC University. Know More
Offices
Know More
Programs in Detail
BRAC University offers a wide range of programs, catering to diverse fields of study. These programs are designed to equip students with the knowledge and skills needed for their chosen careers. Know More
Policies & Procedures
BRAC University follows a model of higher education consisting of semesters, courses, credit hours, continuous evaluation and letter grading. Know More
Institutional Quality Assurance Cell (IQAC)
Quality assurance in tertiary education is a global practice now. The Institutional Quality Assurance Cell (IQAC) of Brac University was established in July 2015 in order to ensure quality education at the tertiary level. Know More
Open Society University Network (OSUN)
BRAC University is the first university from South Asia to be part of the Open Society University Network (OSUN). Know More

BRAC University always pays close attention to convenient approach in terms of Admission Procedures. The application process is online and applicants can apply from the convenience of their home. The entire process of admission goes through an intensely selective method where applicants are asked to follow each phase precisely within the given framework of instructions. To get admitted into BRAC University, applicants must qualify in the admission test (written and an interview) after submitting their online application form. As the admission test is a highly competitive one, applicants are required to perform satisfactorily in the examination in order to secure their seats. Undergraduate Programs

Undergraduate Program
There is a wide range of Undergraduate Programs that BRAC University offers so that prospective students can make their degrees as exclusive as they may desire. At BRAC University, we are committed to assuring academic excellence and a dynamic learning environment in each of our Undergraduate Programs. Our superlative faculty is always ready to support students with the greatest assistance. ​​​​​​
Apply Now
Admission Requirements
Minimum Qualifications (except Bachelor of Pharmacy)
Minimum Qualifications (except Bachelor of Pharmacy)
Particulars
Required Minimum GPA
SSC/Equivalent and HSC/Equivalent (including additional subject)
3.5 in SSC and HSC separately
O-level with five subjects and A-level with two subjects as per the scale of A=5, B=4, C=3, D=2
2.5 in O-Level and A-Level separately
International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IB-DP)
Minimum DP Score of 24
Candidates applying for EEE, ECE, CSE, APE and Physics
HSC/ Equivalent
“B” grade in Physics & Mathematics
A-levels
“C” grade in Physics & Mathematics
Candidates for BSc in Computer Science and BSc in Mathematics
HSC/ Equivalent
“B” grade in Mathematics
A-levels
“C” grade in Mathematics
Candidates who have completed any equivalent 12 years of schooling or High School Diploma (Grades 10–12), or William Carey Academy (Grades 10–12), or International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme(IB-DP), or Ontario Secondary School Diploma (Grades 10–12) from a reputed educational institution are also eligible to apply.

Undergraduate Program
There is a wide range of Undergraduate Programs that BRAC University offers so that prospective students can make their degrees as exclusive as they may desire. At BRAC University, we are committed to assuring academic excellence and a dynamic learning environment in each of our Undergraduate Programs. Our superlative faculty is always ready to support students with the greatest assistance. ​​​​​​
Apply Now
Admission Requirements
Minimum Qualifications (except Bachelor of Pharmacy)
Minimum Qualifications (except Bachelor of Pharmacy)
Particulars
Required Minimum GPA
SSC/Equivalent and HSC/Equivalent (including additional subject)
3.5 in SSC and HSC separately
O-level with five subjects and A-level with two subjects as per the scale of A=5, B=4, C=3, D=2
2.5 in O-Level and A-Level separately
International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IB-DP)
Minimum DP Score of 24
Candidates applying for EEE, ECE, CSE, APE and Physics
HSC/ Equivalent
“B” grade in Physics & Mathematics
A-levels
“C” grade in Physics & Mathematics
Candidates for BSc in Computer Science and BSc in Mathematics
HSC/ Equivalent
“B” grade in Mathematics
A-levels
“C” grade in Mathematics
Candidates who have completed any equivalent 12 years of schooling or High School Diploma (Grades 10–12), or William Carey Academy (Grades 10–12), or International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme(IB-DP), or Ontario Secondary School Diploma (Grades 10–12) from a reputed educational institution are also eligible to apply. Before applying, please contact the BRAC University Admissions Office at
[email protected]
for further guidance. Candidates for BSc in Electronic & Communication Engineering and Electrical & Electronic Engineering who had
Physics and Mathematics but not Chemistry in HSC/A-level/Equivalent will have to take a remedial course on Chemistry in addition to the courses required for the program. Candidates for BSc in Biotechnology and Microbiology should have minimum C grade in Biology and Chemistry in HSC/A-level/Equivalent. Candidates who did not have Mathematics in HSC/A-level/Equivalent must take a remedial course on Mathematics in addition to the courses required for the program. Candidates applying for BSc in EEE, ECE, CSE, APE, and Physics, who have completed IB-DP with a minimum DP Score of 24 [MAT-(HL/SL 5) & PHY-(HL/SL 5)] are eligible to apply. Candidates applying for BSc in Biotechnology and Microbiology, who have completed IB-DP with a minimum DP Score of 24 [BIOLOGY-(HL/SL 5) & CHEMISTRY-(HL/SL 5)] are eligible to apply. Candidates who have qualified in HSC/A levels or recognized equivalent examination in current year or two previous years are eligible to apply. “E” grades will not be considered. Bachelor of Pharmacy
Minimum Qualifications [Public/ Private University B.

A-levels
“C” grade in Mathematics
Candidates who have completed any equivalent 12 years of schooling or High School Diploma (Grades 10–12), or William Carey Academy (Grades 10–12), or International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme(IB-DP), or Ontario Secondary School Diploma (Grades 10–12) from a reputed educational institution are also eligible to apply. Before applying, please contact the BRAC University Admissions Office at
[email protected]
for further guidance. Candidates for BSc in Electronic & Communication Engineering and Electrical & Electronic Engineering who had
Physics and Mathematics but not Chemistry in HSC/A-level/Equivalent will have to take a remedial course on Chemistry in addition to the courses required for the program. Candidates for BSc in Biotechnology and Microbiology should have minimum C grade in Biology and Chemistry in HSC/A-level/Equivalent. Candidates who did not have Mathematics in HSC/A-level/Equivalent must take a remedial course on Mathematics in addition to the courses required for the program. Candidates applying for BSc in EEE, ECE, CSE, APE, and Physics, who have completed IB-DP with a minimum DP Score of 24 [MAT-(HL/SL 5) & PHY-(HL/SL 5)] are eligible to apply. Candidates applying for BSc in Biotechnology and Microbiology, who have completed IB-DP with a minimum DP Score of 24 [BIOLOGY-(HL/SL 5) & CHEMISTRY-(HL/SL 5)] are eligible to apply. Candidates who have qualified in HSC/A levels or recognized equivalent examination in current year or two previous years are eligible to apply. “E” grades will not be considered. Bachelor of Pharmacy
Minimum Qualifications [Public/ Private University B. Pharm Program Accreditation and Student Admission Guideline]
Particulars
Candidates must have a minimum aggregate GPA of 8.00 out of 10 (on a scale of 10), with no grade lower than 3.50 in SSC/O Level/Equivalent and HSC/A Level/Equivalent. In addition, they must achieve a minimum GPA of 3.50 in Chemistry and Biology in both examinations (on a scale of 5). They also need a minimum GPA of 3.00 in Mathematics and Physics in both examinations (on a scale of 5 in HSC or equivalent). For "A" Level candidates, they must have a B grade in Chemistry, Biology, and a minimum C grade in Mathematics and Physics. Candidates not having Mathematics or failing or less than 3.0 in the subject at the HSC/”A” Level or recognized equivalent exam may be admitted, but they need to take an extra 3 (three) credits course on Mathematics relevant to the B.Pharm.

Candidates who have completed any equivalent 12 years of schooling or High School Diploma (Grades 10–12), or William Carey Academy (Grades 10–12), or International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme(IB-DP), or Ontario Secondary School Diploma (Grades 10–12) from a reputed educational institution are also eligible to apply. Before applying, please contact the BRAC University Admissions Office at
[email protected]
for further guidance. Candidates for BSc in Electronic & Communication Engineering and Electrical & Electronic Engineering who had
Physics and Mathematics but not Chemistry in HSC/A-level/Equivalent will have to take a remedial course on Chemistry in addition to the courses required for the program. Candidates for BSc in Biotechnology and Microbiology should have minimum C grade in Biology and Chemistry in HSC/A-level/Equivalent. Candidates who did not have Mathematics in HSC/A-level/Equivalent must take a remedial course on Mathematics in addition to the courses required for the program. Candidates applying for BSc in EEE, ECE, CSE, APE, and Physics, who have completed IB-DP with a minimum DP Score of 24 [MAT-(HL/SL 5) & PHY-(HL/SL 5)] are eligible to apply. Candidates applying for BSc in Biotechnology and Microbiology, who have completed IB-DP with a minimum DP Score of 24 [BIOLOGY-(HL/SL 5) & CHEMISTRY-(HL/SL 5)] are eligible to apply. Candidates who have qualified in HSC/A levels or recognized equivalent examination in current year or two previous years are eligible to apply. “E” grades will not be considered. Bachelor of Pharmacy
Minimum Qualifications [Public/ Private University B. Pharm Program Accreditation and Student Admission Guideline]
Particulars
Candidates must have a minimum aggregate GPA of 8.00 out of 10 (on a scale of 10), with no grade lower than 3.50 in SSC/O Level/Equivalent and HSC/A Level/Equivalent. In addition, they must achieve a minimum GPA of 3.50 in Chemistry and Biology in both examinations (on a scale of 5). They also need a minimum GPA of 3.00 in Mathematics and Physics in both examinations (on a scale of 5 in HSC or equivalent). For "A" Level candidates, they must have a B grade in Chemistry, Biology, and a minimum C grade in Mathematics and Physics. Candidates not having Mathematics or failing or less than 3.0 in the subject at the HSC/”A” Level or recognized equivalent exam may be admitted, but they need to take an extra 3 (three) credits course on Mathematics relevant to the B.Pharm. Curriculum. Applicants for admission to Pharm courses should complete the admission process within 03 (three) years from the year (AD) of publication of HSC/equivalent examination results. The Foreign Certificate holder and the Foreign students who have completed 12 years of education and got the same grades in recognized equivalent examinations from foreign recognized institutions and must get an equivalence certificate from the Pharmacy Council BEFORE ADMISSION. Recognized equivalents mean Education Board, Madrasah Board and Bangladesh Technical Education Board, etc. Candidates who have completed IB-DP with a minimum DP Score of 24 [BIO, CHE (HL/SL 5) and PHY-(HL/SL 4)] are eligible to apply (need equivalence certificate from the Pharmacy Council before admission).

They also need a minimum GPA of 3.00 in Mathematics and Physics in both examinations (on a scale of 5 in HSC or equivalent). For "A" Level candidates, they must have a B grade in Chemistry, Biology, and a minimum C grade in Mathematics and Physics. Candidates not having Mathematics or failing or less than 3.0 in the subject at the HSC/”A” Level or recognized equivalent exam may be admitted, but they need to take an extra 3 (three) credits course on Mathematics relevant to the B.Pharm. Curriculum. Applicants for admission to Pharm courses should complete the admission process within 03 (three) years from the year (AD) of publication of HSC/equivalent examination results. The Foreign Certificate holder and the Foreign students who have completed 12 years of education and got the same grades in recognized equivalent examinations from foreign recognized institutions and must get an equivalence certificate from the Pharmacy Council BEFORE ADMISSION. Recognized equivalents mean Education Board, Madrasah Board and Bangladesh Technical Education Board, etc. Candidates who have completed IB-DP with a minimum DP Score of 24 [BIO, CHE (HL/SL 5) and PHY-(HL/SL 4)] are eligible to apply (need equivalence certificate from the Pharmacy Council before admission). Foundation and GED programs do not qualify for admission to undergraduate programs. Candidates who completed schooling outside Bangladesh will have to submit verified/attested copies of previous academic documents from their Institute/Foreign Ministry (of the country that they studied in) and equivalence certificates from the Board of Intermediate and Secondary Education, Dhaka, Bangladesh
Admission Test
All eligible candidates will have to sit for a written admission test on section/sections as shown in the table:
Program
Test section
BSS in Anthropology, BA in English and LLB (Hons)
English
BBA and BSS in Economics
English and Mathematics
Bachelor of Pharmacy, BSc in Microbiology and BSc in Biotechnology
English, Mathematics and Biology & Chemistry
BSc in Computer Science, BSc in Computer Science and Engineering, BSc in Electronic and Communication Engineering, BSc in Electrical and Electronic Engineering, BSc in Physics, BSc in Applied Physics & Electronics, BSc in Mathematics
English, Mathematics,
Higher Mathematics and Physics
Bachelor of Architecture
English, Mathematics and Drawing
In order to qualify, candidates must pass each sub section separately. Candidates who qualified in pre-university on the basis of written tests, will be called for an interview for final selection. Break of studies
Candidates with a break in studies of up to three years are accepted.

Candidates for BSc in Biotechnology and Microbiology should have minimum C grade in Biology and Chemistry in HSC/A-level/Equivalent. Candidates who did not have Mathematics in HSC/A-level/Equivalent must take a remedial course on Mathematics in addition to the courses required for the program. Candidates applying for BSc in EEE, ECE, CSE, APE, and Physics, who have completed IB-DP with a minimum DP Score of 24 [MAT-(HL/SL 5) & PHY-(HL/SL 5)] are eligible to apply. Candidates applying for BSc in Biotechnology and Microbiology, who have completed IB-DP with a minimum DP Score of 24 [BIOLOGY-(HL/SL 5) & CHEMISTRY-(HL/SL 5)] are eligible to apply. Candidates who have qualified in HSC/A levels or recognized equivalent examination in current year or two previous years are eligible to apply. “E” grades will not be considered. Bachelor of Pharmacy
Minimum Qualifications [Public/ Private University B. Pharm Program Accreditation and Student Admission Guideline]
Particulars
Candidates must have a minimum aggregate GPA of 8.00 out of 10 (on a scale of 10), with no grade lower than 3.50 in SSC/O Level/Equivalent and HSC/A Level/Equivalent. In addition, they must achieve a minimum GPA of 3.50 in Chemistry and Biology in both examinations (on a scale of 5). They also need a minimum GPA of 3.00 in Mathematics and Physics in both examinations (on a scale of 5 in HSC or equivalent). For "A" Level candidates, they must have a B grade in Chemistry, Biology, and a minimum C grade in Mathematics and Physics. Candidates not having Mathematics or failing or less than 3.0 in the subject at the HSC/”A” Level or recognized equivalent exam may be admitted, but they need to take an extra 3 (three) credits course on Mathematics relevant to the B.Pharm. Curriculum. Applicants for admission to Pharm courses should complete the admission process within 03 (three) years from the year (AD) of publication of HSC/equivalent examination results. The Foreign Certificate holder and the Foreign students who have completed 12 years of education and got the same grades in recognized equivalent examinations from foreign recognized institutions and must get an equivalence certificate from the Pharmacy Council BEFORE ADMISSION. Recognized equivalents mean Education Board, Madrasah Board and Bangladesh Technical Education Board, etc. Candidates who have completed IB-DP with a minimum DP Score of 24 [BIO, CHE (HL/SL 5) and PHY-(HL/SL 4)] are eligible to apply (need equivalence certificate from the Pharmacy Council before admission). Foundation and GED programs do not qualify for admission to undergraduate programs. Candidates who completed schooling outside Bangladesh will have to submit verified/attested copies of previous academic documents from their Institute/Foreign Ministry (of the country that they studied in) and equivalence certificates from the Board of Intermediate and Secondary Education, Dhaka, Bangladesh
Admission Test
All eligible candidates will have to sit for a written admission test on section/sections as shown in the table:
Program
Test section
BSS in Anthropology, BA in English and LLB (Hons)
English
BBA and BSS in Economics
English and Mathematics
Bachelor of Pharmacy, BSc in Microbiology and BSc in Biotechnology
English, Mathematics and Biology & Chemistry
BSc in Computer Science, BSc in Computer Science and Engineering, BSc in Electronic and Communication Engineering, BSc in Electrical and Electronic Engineering, BSc in Physics, BSc in Applied Physics & Electronics, BSc in Mathematics
English, Mathematics,
Higher Mathematics and Physics
Bachelor of Architecture
English, Mathematics and Drawing
In order to qualify, candidates must pass each sub section separately. Candidates who qualified in pre-university on the basis of written tests, will be called for an interview for final selection. Break of studies
Candidates with a break in studies of up to three years are accepted. For exceptional cases beyond three years, applicants should email
[email protected]
for review and approval by the Chair of the Admissions Committee. For Pharmacy, Candidate must pass HSC/ "A" level or recognized equivalent examination in current year or one previous year. Credit Transfer
Transfer of credits from an educational institution following a system similar to BRAC University may be considered after passing the admission test. Such candidates will have to apply with required documents and decisions on such cases will be determined by the credit transfer policy of BRAC University.

Undergraduate Program
There is a wide range of Undergraduate Programs that BRAC University offers so that prospective students can make their degrees as exclusive as they may desire. At BRAC University, we are committed to assuring academic excellence and a dynamic learning environment in each of our Undergraduate Programs. Our superlative faculty is always ready to support students with the greatest assistance. ​​​​​​
Apply Now
Admission Requirements
Minimum Qualifications (except Bachelor of Pharmacy)
Minimum Qualifications (except Bachelor of Pharmacy)
Particulars
Required Minimum GPA
SSC/Equivalent and HSC/Equivalent (including additional subject)
3.5 in SSC and HSC separately
O-level with five subjects and A-level with two subjects as per the scale of A=5, B=4, C=3, D=2
2.5 in O-Level and A-Level separately

A-levels
“C” grade in Mathematics
Candidates who have completed any equivalent 12 years of schooling or High School Diploma (Grades 10–12), or William Carey Academy (Grades 10–12), or International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme(IB-DP), or Ontario Secondary School Diploma (Grades 10–12) from a reputed educational institution are also eligible to apply. Before applying, please contact the BRAC University Admissions Office at
[email protected]
for further guidance. Candidates for BSc in Electronic & Communication Engineering and Electrical & Electronic Engineering who had
Physics and Mathematics but not Chemistry in HSC/A-level/Equivalent will have to take a remedial course on Chemistry in addition to the courses required for the program. Candidates for BSc in Biotechnology and Microbiology should have minimum C grade in Biology and Chemistry in HSC/A-level/Equivalent. Candidates who did not have Mathematics in HSC/A-level/Equivalent must take a remedial course on Mathematics in addition to the courses required for the program. Candidates applying for BSc in EEE, ECE, CSE, APE, and Physics, who have completed IB-DP with a minimum DP Score of 24 [MAT-(HL/SL 5) & PHY-(HL/SL 5)] are eligible to apply. Candidates applying for BSc in Biotechnology and Microbiology, who have completed IB-DP with a minimum DP Score of 24 [BIOLOGY-(HL/SL 5) & CHEMISTRY-(HL/SL 5)] are eligible to apply. Candidates who have qualified in HSC/A levels or recognized equivalent examination in current year or two previous years are eligible to apply. “E” grades will not be considered. Bachelor of Pharmacy
Minimum Qualifications [Public/ Private University B. Pharm Program Accreditation and Student Admission Guideline]
Particulars
Candidates must have a minimum aggregate GPA of 8.00 out of 10 (on a scale of 10), with no grade lower than 3.50 in SSC/O Level/Equivalent and HSC/A Level/Equivalent. In addition, they must achieve a minimum GPA of 3.50 in Chemistry and Biology in both examinations (on a scale of 5). They also need a minimum GPA of 3.00 in Mathematics and Physics in both examinations (on a scale of 5 in HSC or equivalent). For "A" Level candidates, they must have a B grade in Chemistry, Biology, and a minimum C grade in Mathematics and Physics. Candidates not having Mathematics or failing or less than 3.0 in the subject at the HSC/”A” Level or recognized equivalent exam may be admitted, but they need to take an extra 3 (three) credits course on Mathematics relevant to the B.Pharm. Curriculum. Applicants for admission to Pharm courses should complete the admission process within 03 (three) years from the year (AD) of publication of HSC/equivalent examination results. The Foreign Certificate holder and the Foreign students who have completed 12 years of education and got the same grades in recognized equivalent examinations from foreign recognized institutions and must get an equivalence certificate from the Pharmacy Council BEFORE ADMISSION. Recognized equivalents mean Education Board, Madrasah Board and Bangladesh Technical Education Board, etc. Candidates who have completed IB-DP with a minimum DP Score of 24 [BIO, CHE (HL/SL 5) and PHY-(HL/SL 4)] are eligible to apply (need equivalence certificate from the Pharmacy Council before admission). Foundation and GED programs do not qualify for admission to undergraduate programs. Candidates who completed schooling outside Bangladesh will have to submit verified/attested copies of previous academic documents from their Institute/Foreign Ministry (of the country that they studied in) and equivalence certificates from the Board of Intermediate and Secondary Education, Dhaka, Bangladesh
Admission Test
All eligible candidates will have to sit for a written admission test on section/sections as shown in the table:
Program
Test section
BSS in Anthropology, BA in English and LLB (Hons)
English
BBA and BSS in Economics
English and Mathematics
Bachelor of Pharmacy, BSc in Microbiology and BSc in Biotechnology
English, Mathematics and Biology & Chemistry
BSc in Computer Science, BSc in Computer Science and Engineering, BSc in Electronic and Communication Engineering, BSc in Electrical and Electronic Engineering, BSc in Physics, BSc in Applied Physics & Electronics, BSc in Mathematics
English, Mathematics,
Higher Mathematics and Physics
Bachelor of Architecture
English, Mathematics and Drawing
In order to qualify, candidates must pass each sub section separately. Candidates who qualified in pre-university on the basis of written tests, will be called for an interview for final selection. Break of studies
Candidates with a break in studies of up to three years are accepted. For exceptional cases beyond three years, applicants should email
[email protected]
for review and approval by the Chair of the Admissions Committee. For Pharmacy, Candidate must pass HSC/ "A" level or recognized equivalent examination in current year or one previous year. Credit Transfer
Transfer of credits from an educational institution following a system similar to BRAC University may be considered after passing the admission test. Such candidates will have to apply with required documents and decisions on such cases will be determined by the credit transfer policy of BRAC University. Visiting Student or Guest Student
Visiting or guest students are required to submit their applications along with the relevant transcripts and no objection letter (NOC) from her/his university subject to approval from chair, admissions committee. Visiting students are not required to appear in the admission test. A visiting student will be able to take a maximum of 2 courses in a semester and a maximum of 8 courses over time. This is to ensure that a visiting student does not take too many courses and apply for a degree at BRAC University.

Home
Student Life
Residential Semester
RS: a unique offering of BRAC University
The Residential Semester (RS) is an important part of the learning experience for each undergraduate student of BRAC University. When the students are in their 3rd semester, they have to go to a specially designed campus in Savar, some 18 kilometers away from capital Dhaka, to complete their General Education courses.

Away from home
BRAC University’s Residential Semester (RS) at Savar campus is unique amongst higher education experiences in Bangladesh. Residential Semester
Home
Student Life
Residential Semester
RS: a unique offering of BRAC University
The Residential Semester (RS) is an important part of the learning experience for each undergraduate student of BRAC University. When the students are in their 3rd semester, they have to go to a specially designed campus in Savar, some 18 kilometers away from capital Dhaka, to complete their General Education courses. Know More
❮
❯
Away from home
BRAC University’s Residential Semester (RS) at Savar campus is unique amongst higher education experiences in Bangladesh. The RS offers a holistic curriculum based on the principle of ‘experiential learning’, which cultivates a broad range of soft skills and qualities, to compliment the theoretical development that students undergo. The curriculum’s intensive programs (delivered mainly in English) help to develop rounded individuals, whose improved leadership skills, ability to independently face challenges, and proficiency in English will better prepare them to face the challenges of higher education (and eventually give them a competitive edge in the job market). The Residential Semester takes place at a specially designed campus in Savar, which provides a support system that aids students in becoming confident and self-reliant. Student Accommodation
The major infrastructure of the RS campus includes dormitory facilities, dining, classroom, hall room, computer lab, common room, library, medical services, psychosocial support and sports facilities. Extra Curricular Activities
To push the students to come up, speak out and flourish their hidden talents, RS offers and organizes, like every semester, many different extra curricular activities at Savar Campus. Rs organises activities like Quiz competition, Wall Magazine, creative writing, drama, Spelling bee and so on. Students can sign up for at least one activity along with their regular course studies. Whatever activities the students are involved in, they are communicating in English with each other, working in groups and nourishing their dormant talent. Student Counseling
Life is full of opportunities and potentialities. Every individual can rejoice the beauty of life and has the capacity to overcome life challenges.

Life is full of opportunities and potentialities. Every individual can rejoice the beauty of life and has the capacity to overcome life challenges. Dining Services
Dining services on the residential campus include a cafeteria that offers a variety of healthy and delicious meal options, catering to diverse dietary needs. For those looking for quick bites and refreshments, convenient snack bars are available throughout the campus. This ensures that students have access to nutritious food and snacks, enhancing their overall living experience. Residential Semester emphasises four areas:
Improving
communications skills
, especially in English. Creating a
strong sense of social responsibility
and an awareness of each other’s rights and responsibilities as members of society and citizens of a nation. Developing
firm principles
that guide students in all of their actions, whether in the personal, social, or public spheres, and teaching them to respect one another. Providing a
holistic education
, through academic, extracurricular and experiential activities.

Residential Semester
Home
Student Life
Residential Semester
RS: a unique offering of BRAC University
The Residential Semester (RS) is an important part of the learning experience for each undergraduate student of BRAC University. When the students are in their 3rd semester, they have to go to a specially designed campus in Savar, some 18 kilometers away from capital Dhaka, to complete their General Education courses. Know More
❮
❯
Away from home
BRAC University’s Residential Semester (RS) at Savar campus is unique amongst higher education experiences in Bangladesh. The RS offers a holistic curriculum based on the principle of ‘experiential learning’, which cultivates a broad range of soft skills and qualities, to compliment the theoretical development that students undergo. The curriculum’s intensive programs (delivered mainly in English) help to develop rounded individuals, whose improved leadership skills, ability to independently face challenges, and proficiency in English will better prepare them to face the challenges of higher education (and eventually give them a competitive edge in the job market). The Residential Semester takes place at a specially designed campus in Savar, which provides a support system that aids students in becoming confident and self-reliant. Student Accommodation
The major infrastructure of the RS campus includes dormitory facilities, dining, classroom, hall room, computer lab, common room, library, medical services, psychosocial support and sports facilities. Extra Curricular Activities
To push the students to come up, speak out and flourish their hidden talents, RS offers and organizes, like every semester, many different extra curricular activities at Savar Campus. Rs organises activities like Quiz competition, Wall Magazine, creative writing, drama, Spelling bee and so on. Students can sign up for at least one activity along with their regular course studies. Whatever activities the students are involved in, they are communicating in English with each other, working in groups and nourishing their dormant talent. Student Counseling
Life is full of opportunities and potentialities. Every individual can rejoice the beauty of life and has the capacity to overcome life challenges. Dining Services
Dining services on the residential campus include a cafeteria that offers a variety of healthy and delicious meal options, catering to diverse dietary needs. For those looking for quick bites and refreshments, convenient snack bars are available throughout the campus. This ensures that students have access to nutritious food and snacks, enhancing their overall living experience. Residential Semester emphasises four areas:
Improving
communications skills
, especially in English. Creating a
strong sense of social responsibility
and an awareness of each other’s rights and responsibilities as members of society and citizens of a nation. Developing
firm principles
that guide students in all of their actions, whether in the personal, social, or public spheres, and teaching them to respect one another. Providing a
holistic education
, through academic, extracurricular and experiential activities. Get prepared for Residential Semester

a. Face mask or hood of any kind that makes the individual unidentified. b. Shorts of any kind, including three-quarter. c. Mini or midi skirts. d. Bathroom slippers (except for cleaning staff). e. A dress item that contains offensive slogan, picture or sign.

a. Face mask or hood of any kind that makes the individual unidentified. b. Shorts of any kind, including three-quarter. c. Mini or midi skirts. d. Bathroom slippers (except for cleaning staff). e.
